revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''鴨習太神社'''（かもならいたじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]にある[[神社]]。[[社格]]は旧村社。 ==祭神...'
revision.text:
'''鴨習太神社'''（かもならいたじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]にある[[神社]]。[[社格]]は旧村社。
==祭神== *[[饒速日命]] *[[高皇産霊尊]]
==由緒== この神社の創建については不詳であるが、[[延喜式]]に記載された[[式内社]]のひとつである。[[江戸時代]]末期までは「天満宮」と称されていたが、[[明治]]に入り現社名に改められた。
==所在地== *大阪府南河内郡河南町神山595
==外部リンク== *[http://kamnavi.jp/mn/osaka/kamonara.htm 鴨習太神社（神奈備）]
[[category:大阪府の神社|かもならいたしんしや]] [[category:河南町|かもならいたしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}]

revision.comment: /* 外部リンク */
revision.text:
'''鴨習太神社'''（かもならいたじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]にある[[神社]]。[[社格]]は旧村社。
==祭神== *[[饒速日命]] *[[高皇産霊尊]]
==由緒== この神社の創建については不詳であるが、[[延喜式]]に記載された[[式内社]]のひとつである。[[江戸時代]]末期までは「天満宮」と称されていたが、[[明治]]に入り現社名に改められた。
==所在地== *大阪府南河内郡河南町神山595
==外部リンク== *[http://kamnavi.jp/mn/osaka/kamonara.htm 鴨習太神社（神奈備）]
[[category:大阪府の神社|かもならいたしんしや]] [[category:河南町|かもならいたしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

revision.comment: fmt,追記
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=鴨習太神社| 所在地=大阪府南河内郡河南町神山595番地| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|48|N|135|37|11|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=饒速日命
天照地照彦火明命
櫛玉命| 社格=式内社（小）・村社| 本殿=[[入母屋造]]| 例祭=10月第3日曜日}} '''鴨習太神社'''（かもならいたじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== 主祭神は饒速日命・天照地照彦火明命・櫛玉命となっているが、いづれも[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]の別名である。一帯が[[物部氏]]の勢力圏であったことから物部氏の[[祖神]]・饒速日命を主祭神としたものであるが、社名に「鴨」とあることから本来の祭神は[[賀茂氏]]に関する神であるとする説もある。 相殿に[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊尊]]を祀る。 ==歴史== 創建の由緒は不詳である。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国石川郡 鴨習太神社」と記載され小社に列しているが、後に衰退し、所在が不明となっていた。 現在の鴨習太神社は、明治時代まで「天満宮」と称し[[菅原道真]]を祀っていた神社である。[[万治]]3年（[[1660年]]）11月、附近にあった高皇産霊尊を祀る天神神山神社の社殿が暴風により破壊されたため、当社に合祀された。式内・鴨習太神社に比定され、現社名に改称し、主祭神を饒速日命に改めた。明治5年に村社に列格した。 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.geocities.jp/engishiki01/kawachi/html/040109-01.html 鴨習太神社]（延喜式神社の調査） *[http://kamnavi.jp/mn/osaka/kamonara.htm 鴨習太神社]（神奈備） {{DEFAULTSORT:かもならいたしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:式内小社]] [[Category:村社]] [[Category:河南町]]

{{神社| 名称=鴨習太神社| 所在地=大阪府南河内郡河南町神山595番地| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|48|N|135|37|11|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=饒速日命

{{神社| 名称=鴨習太神社| 画像=[[Image:Kamonaraita-jinja1.jpg|220px]]| 所在地=大阪府南河内郡河南町神山595番地| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|48|N|135|37|11|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=饒速日命
天照地照彦火明命
櫛玉命| 社格=式内社（小）・村社| 本殿=[[入母屋造]]| 例祭=10月第3日曜日}} '''鴨習太神社'''（かもならいたじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==祭神== 主祭神は饒速日命・天照地照彦火明命・櫛玉命となっているが、いずれも[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]の別名である。一帯が[[物部氏]]の勢力圏であったことから物部氏の[[祖神]]・饒速日命を主祭神としたものであるが、社名に「鴨」とあることから本来の祭神は[[賀茂氏]]に関する神であるとする説もある。 相殿に[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊尊]]を祀る。 ==歴史== 創建の由緒は不詳である。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国石川郡 鴨習太神社」と記載され小社に列しているが、後に衰退し、所在が不明となっていた。 現在の鴨習太神社は、明治時代まで「天満宮」と称し[[菅原道真]]を祀っていた神社である。[[万治]]3年（[[1660年]]）11月、附近にあった高皇産霊尊を祀る天神神山神社の社殿が暴風により破壊されたため、当社に合祀された。式内・鴨習太神社に比定され、現社名に改称し、主祭神を饒速日命に改めた。明治5年に村社に列格した。 {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもならいたしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河南町]]

{{nihongo|'''Kamonaraita Shrine'''|鴨習太神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamonaraita Shrine'''|鴨習太神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamonaraita Shrine'''|鴨習太神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamonaraita Shrine'''|鴨習太神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamonaraita Shrine'''|鴨習太神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Kamonaraita Shrine'''|鴨習太神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

[[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[JA:鴨習太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11675603}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|鴨習太神社|version=107260293|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年5月}} {{神社 | 名称=鴨習太神社 | 画像=[[Image:Kamonaraita-jinja1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]神山595番地 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|48|N|135|37|11|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神=[[饒速日命]]
天照地照彦火明命
櫛玉命 | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] | 本殿=[[入母屋造]] | 例祭=10月第3日曜日 | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=鴨習太神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''鴨習太神社'''（かもならいたじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == 主祭神は饒速日命・天照地照彦火明命・櫛玉命となっているが、いずれも[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]の別名である。一帯が[[物部氏]]の勢力圏であったことから物部氏の[[祖神]]・饒速日命を主祭神としたものであるが、社名に「鴨」とあることから本来の祭神は[[賀茂氏]]に関する神であるとする説もある。 相殿に[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊尊]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == 創建の由緒は不詳であるが、延喜7年より前と思われる。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国石川郡 鴨習太神社」と記載され小社に列しているが、後に衰退し、所在が不明となっていた。 現在の鴨習太神社は、明治時代まで「天満宮」と称し[[菅原道真]]を祀っていた神社である。[[万治]]3年（[[1660年]]）11月、附近にあった高皇産霊尊を祀る天神神山神社の社殿が暴風により破壊されたため、当社に合祀された。式内・鴨習太神社に比定され、現社名に改称し、主祭神を饒速日命に改めた。明治5年に村社に列格した。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもならいたしんしや}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河南町の建築物]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]] ==End Japanese==

[[Category:Kanpei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|鴨習太神社|version=107260293|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年5月}} {{神社 | 名称=鴨習太神社 | 画像=[[Image:Kamonaraita-jinja1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]神山595番地 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|48|N|135|37|11|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神=[[饒速日命]]
天照地照彦火明命
櫛玉命 | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[村社]] | 本殿=[[入母屋造]] | 例祭=10月第3日曜日 | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=鴨習太神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''鴨習太神社'''（かもならいたじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。 == 祭神 == 主祭神は饒速日命・天照地照彦火明命・櫛玉命となっているが、いずれも[[ニギハヤヒ|饒速日命]]の別名である。一帯が[[物部氏]]の勢力圏であったことから物部氏の[[祖神]]・饒速日命を主祭神としたものであるが、社名に「鴨」とあることから本来の祭神は[[賀茂氏]]に関する神であるとする説もある。 相殿に[[タカミムスビ|高皇産霊尊]]を祀る。 == 歴史 == 創建の由緒は不詳であるが、延喜7年より前と思われる。[[延喜式神名帳]]に「河内国石川郡 鴨習太神社」と記載され小社に列しているが、後に衰退し、所在が不明となっていた。 現在の鴨習太神社は、明治時代まで「天満宮」と称し[[菅原道真]]を祀っていた神社である。[[万治]]3年（[[1660年]]）11月、附近にあった高皇産霊尊を祀る天神神山神社の社殿が暴風により破壊されたため、当社に合祀された。式内・鴨習太神社に比定され、現社名に改称し、主祭神を饒速日命に改めた。明治5年に村社に列格した。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河南町の建築物]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]]

旧{{jalink|村社}} | 本殿={{jalink|入母屋造|Q855025}} | 例祭=10月第3日曜日 | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=鴨習太神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''鴨習太神社'''（かもならいたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|南河内郡|Q1347323}}{{jalink|河南町|Q2305508}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社}}。 == 祭神 == 主祭神は饒速日命・天照地照彦火明命・櫛玉命となっているが、いずれも{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|饒速日命}}の別名である。一帯が{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}の勢力圏であったことから物部氏の{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}・饒速日命を主祭神としたものであるが、社名に「鴨」とあることから本来の祭神は{{jalink|賀茂氏|Q122979436}}に関する神であるとする説もある。 相殿に{{jalink|タカミムスビ||高皇産霊尊}}を祀る。 == 歴史 == 創建の由緒は不詳であるが、延喜7年より前と思われる。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「河内国石川郡 鴨習太神社」と記載され小社に列しているが、後に衰退し、所在が不明となっていた。 現在の鴨習太神社は、明治時代まで「天満宮」と称し{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}を祀っていた神社である。{{jalink|万治|Q1145295}}3年（{{jalink|1660年|Q7001}}）11月、附近にあった高皇産霊尊を祀る天神神山神社の社殿が暴風により破壊されたため、当社に合祀された。式内・鴨習太神社に比定され、現社名に改称し、主祭神を饒速日命に改めた。明治5年に村社に列格した。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河南町の建築物]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]]

旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 本殿={{jalink|入母屋造|Q855025}} | 例祭=10月第3日曜日 | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=鴨習太神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''鴨習太神社'''（かもならいたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|南河内郡|Q1347323}}{{jalink|河南町|Q2305508}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === 主祭神は饒速日命・天照地照彦火明命・櫛玉命となっているが、いずれも{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|饒速日命}}の別名である。一帯が{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}の勢力圏であったことから物部氏の{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}・饒速日命を主祭神としたものであるが、社名に「鴨」とあることから本来の祭神は{{jalink|賀茂氏|Q122979436}}に関する神であるとする説もある。 相殿に{{jalink|タカミムスビ||高皇産霊尊}}を祀る。 === 歴史 === 創建の由緒は不詳であるが、延喜7年より前と思われる。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「河内国石川郡 鴨習太神社」と記載され小社に列しているが、後に衰退し、所在が不明となっていた。 現在の鴨習太神社は、明治時代まで「天満宮」と称し{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}を祀っていた神社である。{{jalink|万治|Q1145295}}3年（{{jalink|1660年|Q7001}}）11月、附近にあった高皇産霊尊を祀る天神神山神社の社殿が暴風により破壊されたため、当社に合祀された。式内・鴨習太神社に比定され、現社名に改称し、主祭神を饒速日命に改めた。明治5年に村社に列格した。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもならいたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河南町の建築物]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]]

Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2120105004717 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府南河内郡河南町神山595番地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:鴨習太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11675603}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年5月}} {{神社 | 名称=鴨習太神社 | 画像=[[Image:Kamonaraita-jinja1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|南河内郡|Q1347323}}{{jalink|河南町|Q2305508}}神山595番地 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|48|N|135|37|11|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|饒速日命}}
天照地照彦火明命
櫛玉命 | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 本殿={{jalink|入母屋造|Q855025}} | 例祭=10月第3日曜日 | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=鴨習太神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''鴨習太神社'''（かもならいたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|南河内郡|Q1347323}}{{jalink|河南町|Q2305508}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === 主祭神は饒速日命・天照地照彦火明命・櫛玉命となっているが、いずれも{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|饒速日命}}の別名である。一帯が{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}の勢力圏であったことから物部氏の{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}・饒速日命を主祭神としたものであるが、社名に「鴨」とあることから本来の祭神は{{jalink|賀茂氏|Q122979436}}に関する神であるとする説もある。 相殿に{{jalink|タカミムスビ||高皇産霊尊}}を祀る。 === 歴史 === 創建の由緒は不詳であるが、延喜7年より前と思われる。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「河内国石川郡 鴨習太神社」と記載され小社に列しているが、後に衰退し、所在が不明となっていた。 現在の鴨習太神社は、明治時代まで「天満宮」と称し{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}を祀っていた神社である。{{jalink|万治|Q1145295}}3年（{{jalink|1660年|Q7001}}）11月、附近にあった高皇産霊尊を祀る天神神山神社の社殿が暴風により破壊されたため、当社に合祀された。式内・鴨習太神社に比定され、現社名に改称し、主祭神を饒速日命に改めた。明治5年に村社に列格した。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもならいたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河南町の建築物]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{出典の明記|date=2020年5月}} {{神社 | 名称=鴨習太神社 | 画像=[[Image:Kamonaraita-jinja1.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地={{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|南河内郡|Q1347323}}{{jalink|河南町|Q2305508}}神山595番地 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|48|N|135|37|11|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|饒速日命}}
天照地照彦火明命
櫛玉命 | 社格={{jalink|式内社}}（小）
旧{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}} | 本殿={{jalink|入母屋造|Q855025}} | 例祭=10月第3日曜日 | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=鴨習太神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''鴨習太神社'''（かもならいたじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|大阪府|Q122723}}{{jalink|南河内郡|Q1347323}}{{jalink|河南町|Q2305508}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}で、旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は{{jalink|村社|Q134917284}}。 === 祭神 === 主祭神は饒速日命・天照地照彦火明命・櫛玉命となっているが、いずれも{{jalink|ニギハヤヒ|Q10949054|饒速日命}}の別名である。一帯が{{jalink|物部氏|Q1755836}}の勢力圏であったことから物部氏の{{jalink|祖神|Q85882782}}・饒速日命を主祭神としたものであるが、社名に「鴨」とあることから本来の祭神は{{jalink|賀茂氏|Q122979436}}に関する神であるとする説もある。 相殿に{{jalink|タカミムスビ||高皇産霊尊}}を祀る。 === 歴史 === 創建の由緒は不詳であるが、延喜7年より前と思われる。{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}に「河内国石川郡 鴨習太神社」と記載され小社に列しているが、後に衰退し、所在が不明となっていた。 現在の鴨習太神社は、明治時代まで「天満宮」と称し{{jalink|菅原道真|Q382005}}を祀っていた神社である。{{jalink|万治|Q1145295}}3年（{{jalink|1660年|Q7001}}）11月、附近にあった高皇産霊尊を祀る天神神山神社の社殿が暴風により破壊されたため、当社に合祀された。式内・鴨習太神社に比定され、現社名に改称し、主祭神を饒速日命に改めた。明治5年に村社に列格した。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもならいたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河南町の建築物]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

{{出典の明記|date=2020年5月}} {{神社 | 名称=Kamonaraita Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Kamonaraita-jinja1.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Minamikawachi District|Q1347323}}{{jalink|Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508}} Kamiyama 595 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|48|N|135|37|11|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054}}
Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto
Kushitama-no-Mikoto | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
former {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}} | 本殿={{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} | 例祭=Third Sunday of October | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamonaraita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamonaraita Shrine''' (かもならいたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508}}, {{jalink|Minamikawachi District|Q1347323}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deities are Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto, and Kushitama-no-Mikoto, but all are alternate names of {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054}}. Because this area was within the sphere of the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}}, this is considered a shrine that took the Mononobe ancestral deity Nigihayahi as its main deity. However, because the shrine name contains "Kamo," there is also a theory that the original deity was related to the {{jalink|Kamo clan|Q122979436}}. In the auxiliary sanctuary, {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto}} is enshrined. === History === Its founding background is unknown, but it is thought to date from before Engi 7. It is listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as "Kamonaraita Shrine, Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province" and classified as a minor shrine, but it later declined and its location became unknown. The present Kamonaraita Shrine was a shrine that, until the Meiji period, was called "Tenmangū" and enshrined {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}. In November {{jalink|1660|Q7001}} (Manji 3), because the shrine building of nearby Tenjin Kamiyama Shrine, which enshrined Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto, was destroyed by a storm, it was merged into this shrine. It was identified as the Shikinai Kamonaraita Shrine, renamed to its present shrine name, and the principal deity was changed to Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもならいたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河南町の建築物]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

It is listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as "Kamonaraita Shrine, Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province" and classified as a minor shrine, but it later declined and its location became unknown. The present Kamonaraita Shrine was a shrine that, until the Meiji period, was called "Tenmangū" and enshrined {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}. In November {{jalink|1660|Q7001}} (Manji 3), because the shrine building of nearby Tenjin Kamiyama Shrine, which enshrined Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto, was destroyed by a storm, it was merged into this shrine. It was identified as the Shikinai Kamonaraita Shrine, renamed to its present shrine name, and the principal deity was changed to Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもならいたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河南町の建築物]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:need translation]]

It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deities are Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto, and Kushitama-no-Mikoto, but all are alternate names of {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054}}. However, because the shrine name contains "Kamo," there is also a theory that the original deity was related to the {{jalink|Kamo clan|Q122979436}}. The present Kamonaraita Shrine was a shrine that, until the Meiji period, was called "Tenmangū" and enshrined {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}. In November {{jalink|1660|Q7001}} (Manji 3), because the shrine building of nearby Tenjin Kamiyama Shrine, which enshrined Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto, was destroyed by a storm, it was merged into this shrine. It was identified as the Shikinai Kamonaraita Shrine, renamed to its present shrine name, and the principal deity was changed to Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. former {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}} | 本殿={{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} | 例祭=Third Sunday of October | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamonaraita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamonaraita Shrine''' (かもならいたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508}}, {{jalink|Minamikawachi District|Q1347323}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deities are Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto, and Kushitama-no-Mikoto, but all are alternate names of {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054}}. Because this area was within the sphere of the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}}, this is considered a shrine that took the Mononobe ancestral deity Nigihayahi as its main deity. However, because the shrine name contains "Kamo," there is also a theory that the original deity was related to the {{jalink|Kamo clan|Q122979436}}. In the auxiliary sanctuary, {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto}} is enshrined. === History === Its founding background is unknown, but it is thought to date from before Engi 7. It is listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as "Kamonaraita Shrine, Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province" and classified as a minor shrine, but it later declined and its location became unknown. The present Kamonaraita Shrine was a shrine that, until the Meiji period, was called "Tenmangū" and enshrined {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}. In November {{jalink|1660|Q7001}} (Manji 3), because the shrine building of nearby Tenjin Kamiyama Shrine, which enshrined Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto, was destroyed by a storm, it was merged into this shrine. It was identified as the Shikinai Kamonaraita Shrine, renamed to its present shrine name, and the principal deity was changed to Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもならいたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河南町の建築物]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]]

{{出典の明記|date=2020年5月}} {{神社 | 名称=Kamonaraita Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Kamonaraita-jinja1.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall | 所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Minamikawachi District|Q1347323}}{{jalink|Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508}} Kamiyama 595 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|48|N|135|37|11|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054}}
Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto
Kushitama-no-Mikoto | 社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (minor)
former {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}} | 本殿={{jalink|irimoya-zukuri|Q855025}} | 例祭=Third Sunday of October | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamonaraita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamonaraita Shrine''' (かもならいたじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508}}, {{jalink|Minamikawachi District|Q1347323}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and under the former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} system it was a {{jalink|Sonsha|Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deities are Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto, and Kushitama-no-Mikoto, but all are alternate names of {{jalink|Nigihayahi|Q10949054}}. Because this area was within the sphere of the {{jalink|Mononobe clan|Q1755836}}, this is considered a shrine that took the Mononobe ancestral deity Nigihayahi as its main deity. However, because the shrine name contains "Kamo," there is also a theory that the original deity was related to the {{jalink|Kamo clan|Q122979436}}. In the auxiliary sanctuary, {{jalink|Takamimusubi||Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto}} is enshrined. === History === Its founding background is unknown, but it is thought to date from before Engi 7. It is listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}} as "Kamonaraita Shrine, Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province" and classified as a minor shrine, but it later declined and its location became unknown. The present Kamonaraita Shrine was a shrine that, until the Meiji period, was called "Tenmangū" and enshrined {{jalink|Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005}}. In November {{jalink|1660|Q7001}} (Manji 3), because the shrine building of nearby Tenjin Kamiyama Shrine, which enshrined Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto, was destroyed by a storm, it was merged into this shrine. It was identified as the Shikinai Kamonaraita Shrine, renamed to its present shrine name, and the principal deity was changed to Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもならいたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河南町の建築物]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]]

{{出典の明記|date=2020年5月}} {{神社 | 名称=Kamonaraita Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Kamonaraita-jinja1.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}{{ill|lt=Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508|WD=Q2305508}} Kamiyama 595 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|48|N|135|37|11|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054}}
Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto
Kushitama-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} (minor)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} | 例祭=Third Sunday of October | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamonaraita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamonaraita Shrine''' (かもならいたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508|WD=Q2305508}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deities are Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto, and Kushitama-no-Mikoto, but all are alternate names of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054}}. Because this area was within the sphere of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}, this is considered a shrine that took the Mononobe ancestral deity Nigihayahi as its main deity. However, because the shrine name contains "Kamo," there is also a theory that the original deity was related to the {{ill|lt=Kamo clan|Q122979436|WD=Q122979436}}. In the auxiliary sanctuary, {{ill|lt=Takamimusubi||WD=|lt=Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto}} is enshrined. === History === Its founding background is unknown, but it is thought to date from before Engi 7. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as "Kamonaraita Shrine, Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province" and classified as a minor shrine, but it later declined and its location became unknown. The present Kamonaraita Shrine was a shrine that, until the Meiji period, was called "Tenmangū" and enshrined {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. In November {{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001}} (Manji 3), because the shrine building of nearby Tenjin Kamiyama Shrine, which enshrined Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto, was destroyed by a storm, it was merged into this shrine. It was identified as the Shikinai Kamonaraita Shrine, renamed to its present shrine name, and the principal deity was changed to Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもならいたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河南町の建築物]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもならいたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河南町の建築物]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{出典の明記|date=2020年5月}} {{神社 | 名称=Kamonaraita Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Kamonaraita-jinja1.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}{{ill|lt=Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508|WD=Q2305508}} Kamiyama 595 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|48|N|135|37|11|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054}}
Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto
Kushitama-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (minor)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} | 例祭=Third Sunday of October | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamonaraita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamonaraita Shrine''' (かもならいたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508|WD=Q2305508}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deities are Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto, and Kushitama-no-Mikoto, but all are alternate names of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054}}. Because this area was within the sphere of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}, this is considered a shrine that took the Mononobe ancestral deity Nigihayahi as its main deity. However, because the shrine name contains "Kamo," there is also a theory that the original deity was related to the {{ill|lt=Kamo clan|Q122979436|WD=Q122979436}}. In the auxiliary sanctuary, {{ill|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto}} is enshrined. === History === Its founding background is unknown, but it is thought to date from before Engi 7. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as "Kamonaraita Shrine, Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province" and classified as a minor shrine, but it later declined and its location became unknown. The present Kamonaraita Shrine was a shrine that, until the Meiji period, was called "Tenmangū" and enshrined {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. In November {{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001}} (Manji 3), because the shrine building of nearby Tenjin Kamiyama Shrine, which enshrined Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto, was destroyed by a storm, it was merged into this shrine. It was identified as the Shikinai Kamonaraita Shrine, renamed to its present shrine name, and the principal deity was changed to Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもならいたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河南町の建築物]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Worship hall | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}{{ill|lt=Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508|WD=Q2305508}} Kamiyama 595 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|48|N|135|37|11|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054}}
Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto
Kushitama-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025}} | 例祭=Third Sunday of October | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamonaraita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamonaraita Shrine''' (かもならいたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508|WD=Q2305508}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deities are Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto, and Kushitama-no-Mikoto, but all are alternate names of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054}}. Because this area was within the sphere of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836}}, this is considered a shrine that took the Mononobe ancestral deity Nigihayahi as its main deity. However, because the shrine name contains "Kamo," there is also a theory that the original deity was related to the {{ill|lt=Kamo clan|Q122979436|WD=Q122979436}}. In the auxiliary sanctuary, {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto}} is enshrined. === History === Its founding background is unknown, but it is thought to date from before Engi 7. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}} as "Kamonaraita Shrine, Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province" and classified as a minor shrine, but it later declined and its location became unknown. The present Kamonaraita Shrine was a shrine that, until the Meiji period, was called "Tenmangū" and enshrined {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005}}. In November {{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001}} (Manji 3), because the shrine building of nearby Tenjin Kamiyama Shrine, which enshrined Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto, was destroyed by a storm, it was merged into this shrine. It was identified as the Shikinai Kamonaraita Shrine, renamed to its present shrine name, and the principal deity was changed to Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもならいたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河南町の建築物]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

(P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府南河内郡河南町神山595番地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:鴨習太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11675603}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年5月}} {{神社 | 名称=Kamonaraita Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Kamonaraita-jinja1.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508|WD=Q2305508|1=Kanan, Osaka}} Kamiyama 595 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|48|N|135|37|11|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}}
Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto
Kushitama-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭=Third Sunday of October | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamonaraita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamonaraita Shrine''' (かもならいたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508|WD=Q2305508|1=Kanan, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deities are Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto, and Kushitama-no-Mikoto, but all are alternate names of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}}. Because this area was within the sphere of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, this is considered a shrine that took the Mononobe ancestral deity Nigihayahi as its main deity. However, because the shrine name contains "Kamo," there is also a theory that the original deity was related to the {{ill|lt=Kamo clan|Q122979436|WD=Q122979436|1=Kamo clan}}. In the auxiliary sanctuary, {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} is enshrined. === History === Its founding background is unknown, but it is thought to date from before Engi 7. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamonaraita Shrine, Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province" and classified as a minor shrine, but it later declined and its location became unknown. The present Kamonaraita Shrine was a shrine that, until the Meiji period, was called "Tenmangū" and enshrined {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. In November {{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001|1=1660}} (Manji 3), because the shrine building of nearby Tenjin Kamiyama Shrine, which enshrined Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto, was destroyed by a storm, it was merged into this shrine. It was identified as the Shikinai Kamonaraita Shrine, renamed to its present shrine name, and the principal deity was changed to Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもならいたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河南町の建築物]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

画像=[[Image:Kamonaraita-jinja1.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508|WD=Q2305508|1=Kanan, Osaka}} Kamiyama 595 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|48|N|135|37|11|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}}
Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto
Kushitama-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭=Third Sunday of October | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamonaraita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamonaraita Shrine''' (かもならいたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508|WD=Q2305508|1=Kanan, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deities are Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto, and Kushitama-no-Mikoto, but all are alternate names of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}}. Because this area was within the sphere of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, this is considered a shrine that took the Mononobe ancestral deity Nigihayahi as its main deity. However, because the shrine name contains "Kamo," there is also a theory that the original deity was related to the {{ill|lt=Kamo clan|Q122979436|WD=Q122979436|1=Kamo clan}}. In the auxiliary sanctuary, {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} is enshrined. === History === Its founding background is unknown, but it is thought to date from before Engi 7. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamonaraita Shrine, Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province" and classified as a minor shrine, but it later declined and its location became unknown. The present Kamonaraita Shrine was a shrine that, until the Meiji period, was called "Tenmangū" and enshrined {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. In November {{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001|1=1660}} (Manji 3), because the shrine building of nearby Tenjin Kamiyama Shrine, which enshrined Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto, was destroyed by a storm, it was merged into this shrine. It was identified as the Shikinai Kamonaraita Shrine, renamed to its present shrine name, and the principal deity was changed to Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かもならいたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧村社]] [[Category:河南町の建築物]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

In the auxiliary sanctuary, {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} is enshrined. === History === Its founding background is unknown, but it is thought to date from before Engi 7. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamonaraita Shrine, Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province" and classified as a minor shrine, but it later declined and its location became unknown. The present Kamonaraita Shrine was a shrine that, until the Meiji period, was called "Tenmangū" and enshrined {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. In November {{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001|1=1660}} (Manji 3), because the shrine building of nearby Tenjin Kamiyama Shrine, which enshrined Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto, was destroyed by a storm, it was merged into this shrine. It was identified as the Shikinai Kamonaraita Shrine, renamed to its present shrine name, and the principal deity was changed to Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:河南町の建築物]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

画像=[[Image:Kamonaraita-jinja1.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508|WD=Q2305508|1=Kanan, Osaka}} Kamiyama 595 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|48|N|135|37|11|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}}
Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto
Kushitama-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭=Third Sunday of October | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamonaraita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamonaraita Shrine''' (かもならいたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508|WD=Q2305508|1=Kanan, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deities are Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto, and Kushitama-no-Mikoto, but all are alternate names of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}}. Because this area was within the sphere of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, this is considered a shrine that took the Mononobe ancestral deity Nigihayahi as its main deity. However, because the shrine name contains "Kamo," there is also a theory that the original deity was related to the {{ill|lt=Kamo clan|Q122979436|WD=Q122979436|1=Kamo clan}}. In the auxiliary sanctuary, {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} is enshrined. === History === Its founding background is unknown, but it is thought to date from before Engi 7. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamonaraita Shrine, Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province" and classified as a minor shrine, but it later declined and its location became unknown. The present Kamonaraita Shrine was a shrine that, until the Meiji period, was called "Tenmangū" and enshrined {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. In November {{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001|1=1660}} (Manji 3), because the shrine building of nearby Tenjin Kamiyama Shrine, which enshrined Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto, was destroyed by a storm, it was merged into this shrine. It was identified as the Shikinai Kamonaraita Shrine, renamed to its present shrine name, and the principal deity was changed to Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:河南町の歴史]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:nnnnnnnnn]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Kushitama-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭=Third Sunday of October | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamonaraita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamonaraita Shrine''' (かもならいたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508|WD=Q2305508|1=Kanan, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deities are Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto, and Kushitama-no-Mikoto, but all are alternate names of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}}. Because this area was within the sphere of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, this is considered a shrine that took the Mononobe ancestral deity Nigihayahi as its main deity. However, because the shrine name contains "Kamo," there is also a theory that the original deity was related to the {{ill|lt=Kamo clan|Q122979436|WD=Q122979436|1=Kamo clan}}. In the auxiliary sanctuary, {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} is enshrined. === History === Its founding background is unknown, but it is thought to date from before Engi 7. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamonaraita Shrine, Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province" and classified as a minor shrine, but it later declined and its location became unknown. The present Kamonaraita Shrine was a shrine that, until the Meiji period, was called "Tenmangū" and enshrined {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. In November {{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001|1=1660}} (Manji 3), because the shrine building of nearby Tenjin Kamiyama Shrine, which enshrined Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto, was destroyed by a storm, it was merged into this shrine. It was identified as the Shikinai Kamonaraita Shrine, renamed to its present shrine name, and the principal deity was changed to Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kanan]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

It is located in [[Kawachi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická (大阪府)|WD=Q11586399|1=Ishikawa district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.480271|135.619595}} == image (P18) == * Kamonaraita-jinja1.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Shinto Directive|lt=Shinto Directive|en|Shinto Directive|fa|بخشنامه شینتو|fr|Directive shinto|id|Pengarahan Shinto|ja|神道指令|zh|神道指令|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == architectural style (P149) == * {{ill|Q855025|ltq=Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2120105004717 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府南河内郡河南町神山595番地', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:鴨習太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11675603}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年5月}} {{神社 | 名称=Kamonaraita Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Kamonaraita-jinja1.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508|WD=Q2305508|1=Kanan, Osaka}} Kamiyama 595 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|48|N|135|37|11|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}}
Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto
Kushitama-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|WD=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭=Third Sunday of October | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamonaraita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamonaraita Shrine''' (かもならいたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508|WD=Q2305508|1=Kanan, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|WD=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deities are Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto, and Kushitama-no-Mikoto, but all are alternate names of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|WD=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}}. Because this area was within the sphere of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|WD=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, this is considered a shrine that took the Mononobe ancestral deity Nigihayahi as its main deity. However, because the shrine name contains "Kamo," there is also a theory that the original deity was related to the {{ill|lt=Kamo clan|Q122979436|WD=Q122979436|1=Kamo clan}}. In the auxiliary sanctuary, {{ill|WD=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} is enshrined. === History === Its founding background is unknown, but it is thought to date from before Engi 7. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamonaraita Shrine, Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province" and classified as a minor shrine, but it later declined and its location became unknown. The present Kamonaraita Shrine was a shrine that, until the Meiji period, was called "Tenmangū" and enshrined {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. In November {{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|WD=Q7001|1=1660}} (Manji 3), because the shrine building of nearby Tenjin Kamiyama Shrine, which enshrined Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto, was destroyed by a storm, it was merged into this shrine. It was identified as the Shikinai Kamonaraita Shrine, renamed to its present shrine name, and the principal deity was changed to Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kanan]]

link|Q11675603}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2020年5月}} {{神社 | 名称=Kamonaraita Shrine | 画像=[[Image:Kamonaraita-jinja1.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall | 所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|qid=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508|qid=Q2305508|1=Kanan, Osaka}} Kamiyama 595 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|28|48|N|135|37|11|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神={{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|qid=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}}
Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto
Kushitama-no-Mikoto | 社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
former {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} | 本殿={{ill|lt=irimoya-zukuri|Q855025|qid=Q855025|1=East Asian hip-and-gable roof}} | 例祭=Third Sunday of October | 地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Kamonaraita Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kamonaraita Shrine''' (かもならいたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kanan, Osaka|Q2305508|qid=Q2305508|1=Kanan, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Minamikawachi District|Q1347323|qid=Q1347323|1=Minamikawachi District, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} system it was a {{ill|lt=Sonsha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === The principal enshrined deities are Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, Amasu Teru Chiteru Hikohomino-Mikoto, and Kushitama-no-Mikoto, but all are alternate names of {{ill|lt=Nigihayahi|Q10949054|qid=Q10949054|1=Nigihayahi}}. Because this area was within the sphere of the {{ill|lt=Mononobe clan|Q1755836|qid=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}, this is considered a shrine that took the Mononobe ancestral deity Nigihayahi as its main deity. However, because the shrine name contains "Kamo," there is also a theory that the original deity was related to the {{ill|lt=Kamo clan|Q122979436|qid=Q122979436|1=Kamo clan}}. In the auxiliary sanctuary, {{ill|qid=Q125400|lt=Takamimusubi|UNKNOWN|lt=Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto|1=Takamimusubi}} is enshrined. === History === Its founding background is unknown, but it is thought to date from before Engi 7. It is listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Kamonaraita Shrine, Ishikawa District, Kawachi Province" and classified as a minor shrine, but it later declined and its location became unknown. The present Kamonaraita Shrine was a shrine that, until the Meiji period, was called "Tenmangū" and enshrined {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}}. In November {{ill|lt=1660|Q7001|qid=Q7001|1=1660}} (Manji 3), because the shrine building of nearby Tenjin Kamiyama Shrine, which enshrined Taka-mi-musubi no Mikoto, was destroyed by a storm, it was merged into this shrine. It was identified as the Shikinai Kamonaraita Shrine, renamed to its present shrine name, and the principal deity was changed to Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. In Meiji 5, it was ranked as a village shrine. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{commons|Category:Kamonaraita-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kamonaraita Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kanan, Osaka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kanan]]
